title: refund — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for resolving refunds, computing refundable amounts, computing proration, and reading approval state. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic.

helpers.purpose: Cache-aware lookup for a refund within a tenant. Returns the Refund model or null when unknown / soft-deleted.

Returns the remaining refundable amount for a payment: payment.amount_cents - SUM(amount_cents) across all NON-cancelled + NON-rejected + NON-failed refunds. Cached via rfd:refundable:{payment_id} (30s TTL).
Pure computation of the requires_approval flag for a proposed refund. Reads config thresholds + reason categories. Used by preview + create paths to give the caller early feedback.

helpers.signature: compute_proration(Membership $membership, string $cancellationDate): array{refund_amount_cents: int, unused_period_start: string, unused_period_end: string, total_period_days: int, unused_days: int, unused_pass_count: int, unused_pass_credit_cents: int}
helpers.purpose: Given a membership + cancellation date, computes the prorated refund amount + freezes proration_config for the refund_line. Read-only. Called by preview + refund_line creation.
Generate the next sequential refund number for a tenant + year. Uses the rfd:sequence:{tenant_id}:{year} counter. Returns a formatted string like 'REF-2026-00000042'.
Returns the list of user_ids holding refunds.approve permission for a tenant. --excludeUserId filters the initiator out (segregation of duties). Cached via rfd:approvers:{tenant_id} (10min TTL).

Returns true when the customer can request a refund on this payment: (1) tenant holds refund_customer_self_service entitlement, (2) customer is the payment's customer (matches payment.customer_type + customer_id), (3) refundable_amount > 0, (4) no active refund is already pending on this payment.
Returns the SUM of amount_cents across all refund_lines for a given refund_id. Used by the sum-invariant observer check + the refund detail view.

Returns true when actor can cancel the refund: (1) status IN ('awaiting_approval', 'approved'), (2) actor is initiator OR has refunds.cancel permission. Used by SDUI to enable/disable the cancel button.

Returns true when the reason IS IN the admin-only set (fraud_confirmed / admin_correction / regulator_required / accounting_correction / chargeback_prevention). Customer path refuses these reasons at request time.
Returns true when the reason IS IN the always-approve set (see is_admin_only_reason set + config refund.reason.always_require_approval).

Returns the configured polling interval in seconds for a specific provider (from config refund.providers.<provider>.polling_interval_seconds). Used by scheduler to dispatch CheckRefundProviderStatusJob at the right cadence.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services.
refundable_amount() is intentionally a `read` — the atomic write path is RefundObserver.creating which acquires row locks via SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the parent payment.
compute_proration() is a pure read — it does NOT freeze the proration_config on any row. Freezing happens at refund_line creation time via ProrationCalculator::freeze().
